en.extract:
The green white-eye (Zosterops stuhlmanni) is a bird species in the family Zosteropidae. It is found in Tanzania, Uganda and the Democratic Republic of the Congo.
The green white-eye was formerly treated as a subspecies of the African yellow white-eye (since renamed the northern yellow white-eye) (Zosterops senegalensis). It is now considered as a separate species based in part on the phylogenetic relationships determined in a 2013 molecular study.
Four subspecies are recognised:

Z. s. stuhlmanni Reichenow, 1892 – northwest Tanzania, south and central Uganda
Z. s. reichenowi Dubois, AJC, 1911 – east D.R. Congo
Z. s. toroensis Reichenow, 1904 – northeast D.R. Congo and west Uganda
Z. s. scotti Neumann, 1899 – southwest Uganda: Bwindi Impenetrable National Park and above 3,000 m (9,800 ft) in the Virunga Mountains

{{for|Zosterops virens virens|Cape white-eye}} 
The '''green white-eye''' ('''''Zosterops stuhlmanni''''') is a bird [[species]] in the family [[Zosteropidae]]. It is found in Tanzania, Uganda and the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

The green white-eye was formerly treated as a [[subspecies]] of the African yellow white-eye (since renamed the [[northern yellow white-eye]]) (''Zosterops senegalensis''). It is now considered as a separate species based in part on the [[phylogenetic]] relationships determined in a 2013 molecular study.<ref>{{ cite thesis | last=Cox | first=S.C. | year=2013 | title=Molecular Systematics and Diversification of African Zosteropidae (Aves: Passeriformes) | type=PhD | institution=University College London | url=http://discovery.ucl.ac.uk/1397762/ }}</ref><ref>{{ cite journal | last1=Cox  | first1=S.C. | last2=Prys-Jones | first2=R.P. | last3=Habel | first3=J.C. | last4=Amakobe  | first4=B.A. | last5=Day | first5=J.J. | year=2014 | title=Niche divergence promotes rapid diversification of East African sky island white-eyes (Aves: Zosteropidae) | journal=Molecular Ecology | volume=23 | issue=16 | pages=4103–4118 | doi=10.1111/mec.12840 | doi-access=free | pmid=24954273 | pmc=4255762 | bibcode=2014MolEc..23.4103C }}</ref><ref>{{ cite journal | last1=Pearson | first1=D.J. | last2=Turner | first2=D.A. | year=2017 | title=A taxonomic review of the genus ''Zosterops'' in East Africa, with a revised list of species occurring in Kenya, Uganda and Tanzania | journal=Scopus  | volume=37 | pages=1–13 | url=https://www.ajol.info/index.php/scopus/article/viewFile/150534/140112 }}</ref><ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | year=2019 | title=Sylviid babblers, parrotbills, white-eyes | work=World Bird List Version 9.1 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/sylvias/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=25 January 2019 }}</ref>

Four [[subspecies]] are recognised:<ref name=ioc/>
* ''Z. s. stuhlmanni'' [[Anton Reichenow|Reichenow]], 1892 – northwest Tanzania, south and central Uganda
* ''Z. s. reichenowi'' [[Alphonse Joseph Charles Dubois|Dubois, AJC]], 1911 – east D.R. Congo
* ''Z. s. toroensis'' Reichenow, 1904 – northeast D.R. Congo and west Uganda
* ''Z. s. scotti'' [[Oscar Neumann|Neumann]], 1899 – southwest Uganda: [[Bwindi Impenetrable National Park]] and above {{cvt|3000|m}} in the [[Virunga Mountains]]
